task_completion.description: Verify the forensic case is created with the correct name and number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Autopsy GUI (or similar forensic tool) showing an open case named 'Carving_Analysis_2024' with the case number 'INV-CARV-006'.

task_completion.description: Verify the disk image was added and processed with the required ingest modules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the data source '/home/ga/evidence/jpeg_search.dd' loaded in the forensic tool's tree view, and evidence that PhotoRec Carver, File Type Identification, and Hash Lookup were run (e.g., a '$carved' directory, ingest module progress bars, or carved files listed in the results).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation and format of the carved files catalog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Reports/carved_files_catalog.txt'. The content must be formatted as pipe-separated values: FILENAME|FILE_SIZE_BYTES|MD5_HASH|CARVED_FROM_OFFSET.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation and format of the allocated non-system files catalog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Reports/allocated_files_catalog.txt'. The content must be formatted as pipe-separated values: FILENAME|FILE_SIZE_BYTES|MD5_HASH|FULL_PATH.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the final carving analysis report with all required sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal displaying '/home/ga/Reports/carving_analysis_report.txt'. It must contain the exact uppercase labels on separate lines: CASE_NAME, CASE_NUMBER, DATA_SOURCE, TOTAL_ALLOCATED_FILES, TOTAL_CARVED_FILES, CARVED_DUPLICATES, CARVED_UNIQUE, CARVING_METHOD, and FORENSIC_ASSESSMENT (with a 1-3 sentence assessment).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual forensic GUI (Autopsy) to process the image and run the ingest modules.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing interaction with the Autopsy graphical interface, specifically the ingest module configuration or the data explorer, rather than just running standalone command-line tools or scripts to bypass the forensic software.

integrity.description: Verify the agent extracted real data for the catalogs rather than hallucinating values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the catalog files to ensure the MD5 hashes are valid 32-character hexadecimal strings and the file sizes/offsets appear to be genuine numbers extracted from the tool, not placeholders like 'HASH_HERE' or '12345'.

integrity.description: Verify the analysis report contains actual calculated numbers for the file counts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the carving_analysis_report.txt to ensure the fields TOTAL_ALLOCATED_FILES, TOTAL_CARVED_FILES, CARVED_DUPLICATES, and CARVED_UNIQUE contain actual integer values resulting from the agent's comparison, rather than being left blank or containing prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Case Name: Carving_Analysis_2024. Disk Image: /home/ga/evidence/jpeg_search.dd. Expected output files: /home/ga/Reports/carved_files_catalog.txt, /home/ga/Reports/allocated_files_catalog.txt, and /home/ga/Reports/carving_analysis_report.txt. The forensic tool expected to be used is Autopsy (which uses an autopsy.db SQLite database to store case data). The ground truth for file counts and hashes is dynamically calculated from this database, so the agent must genuinely process the image through Autopsy's ingest modules (PhotoRec Carver, File Type Identification, Hash Lookup).
